paper.title: Injury profile among elite male youth soccer players in a Swedish first league
paper.fileName: Nilsson - 2016 - Injury profile among elite male youth soccer players in a Swedish first league..pdf

contextFields.label: Incidence Definition
contextFields.value: number of injuries per 1,000 hr of exposure in training and matches

contextFields.label: Severity Definition
contextFields.value: minimal injury (1–3 days absence), minor injury (4–7 days absence), moderate injury (8-28 days absence) and major/severe injury (>28 days absence)

contextFields.label: Title
contextFields.value: Injury profile among elite male youth soccer players in a Swedish first league

injuryOutcome:injuryPlayersCompletedStud.label: Number of players that completed the study

injuryOutcome:injuryTeamsCompletedStudy.label: Number of teams that completed the study

chunks.label: Injury outcome
chunks.description: Extract the full injury outcome section in a single pass.

draftFields.sourceQuote: In total, 61 injuries were recorded during the 2013–2014 seasons...
draftFields.label: Total number of injuries

draftFields.sourceQuote: In total, 43 young elite male soccer players, aged 15 to 19 yr, were prospectively followed...
draftFields.label: Number of players that completed the study

draftFields.sourceQuote: This study investigated the injury profile among elite male youth soccer players in a Swedish first league during two seasons.
draftFields.label: Number of teams that completed the study

draftFields.sourceQuote: The injury definition used in this study followed recommendations made by the International Injury Consensus Group; injuries resulting in the player being unable to fully participate in future soccer training or match was classified as time-loss injury (Fuller et al., 2006).
draftFields.label: Total time-loss injuries

draftFields.sourceQuote: In sum, 18 injuries occurred during matches and 43 injuries occurred during training (Table 1).
draftFields.label: Total match injuries

draftFields.sourceQuote: In sum, 18 injuries occurred during matches and 43 injuries occurred during training (Table 1).
draftFields.label: Total training injuries

draftFields.sourceQuote: The overall incidence of injury in the present study was estimated to 6.8 injuries per 1,000 exposure hours...
draftFields.label: Injury incidence (overall)

draftFields.sourceQuote: ...and 15.5 and 5.6 injuries per 1,000 hr for matches and training, respectively.
draftFields.label: Match injury incidence

draftFields.sourceQuote: ...and 15.5 and 5.6 injuries per 1,000 hr for matches and training, respectively.
draftFields.label: Training injury incidence

draftFields.sourceQuote: The single most common injury subtype was muscle strain (53%).
draftFields.label: Most common injury diagnosis

draftFields.sourceQuote: The single most common injury subtype was muscle strain (53%).
draftFields.label: Most common injury type

draftFields.sourceQuote: The hip and groin were the most common locations for injuries.
draftFields.label: Most common injury location

draftFields.sourceQuote: Of all registered injuries, 41% (25 of 61) of the injuries were estimated as a moderate injury...
draftFields.label: Most common injury severity class
